articles.title: Allworth Financial LP Sells 174,094 Shares of AT&T Inc. $T
articles.summary: Allworth Financial LP significantly reduced its stake in AT&T Inc. by selling 174,094 shares, now holding 1,592,002 shares valued at approximately $44.96 million. The company's recent earnings beat expectations with an EPS of $0.52 and revenue of $33.47 billion, and it provided a positive FY2026 guidance. Analysts currently rate AT&T as a "Moderate Buy" with a consensus price target of $30.35, despite recent stock performance showing some volatility and institutional ownership around 57.10%.

articles.title: AIA Group Ltd Boosts Holdings in AT&T Inc. $T
articles.summary: AIA Group Ltd significantly increased its stake in AT&T Inc. during Q3, raising its holdings by 89.9% to 437,086 shares valued at $12.34 million. This move is part of a broader trend, with several other institutional investors also increasing their positions, bringing total institutional ownership to 57.10%. Wall Street analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating for AT&T, with an average price target of $30.35, following a recent earnings beat and positive FY2026 guidance.

articles.title: Target Corp shares jump 6% to 11-month high as company lifts sales forecast after another weak quarter
articles.summary: Shares of Target Corp rose significantly, hitting an 11-month high, despite the company reporting another weak quarter. This jump was primarily driven by Target's increased sales forecast, signaling investor confidence in future performance despite recent challenges.

articles.title: Target Corp. stock outperforms competitors despite losses on the day
articles.summary: Target Corp. (TGT) shares dropped 1.46% to $115.05 on Wednesday, marking its third consecutive day of losses. Despite the decline, Target outperformed the broader market, as the S&P 500 Index fell 1.36% and the Dow Jones Industrial Average dropped 1.63% during the same trading session.

articles.title: Target Corp. (TGT) jumps 6.7% as firm turns to AI for growth
articles.summary: Target (TGT) saw its stock jump by 6.7% as the company focuses on artificial intelligence to drive growth. This move indicates a strategic shift towards leveraging technology for future expansion and improved operations.
